description: Roadmap #7 — the governance surface is itself budgeted. A per-category cap on the framework's own enforcement machinery (gates, CLI verbs, audit checks) so it can't grow faster than dead surface retires — the cure for F3/F4 becoming the next F3/F4. To exceed a cap, either retire/merge something OR add a waiver row here with a reason (each waiver raises that category's effective cap by exactly one, and shows as visible debt in `maddu audit`). Counts are read from ground truth (discoverGates / bin COMMANDS / the audit's own check registry) — nothing is hand-maintained. Enforced by checkGovernanceBudget in commands/audit.mjs via template/maddu/runtime/lib/governance-budget.mjs.

gates.note: builtin enforcement gates (template/maddu/runtime/gates/builtin/*.mjs). Raised 73→74: +discipline-observed, the self-discipline surfacing gate — reports whether Máddu's session rituals are current (session/lane/goal-or-plan present, slice-stops fresh, work committed) and, critically, whether the PreToolUse enforcement hook is actually installed for the configured governance mode (Codex flagged that a repo can configure strict enforcement yet never wire the hook, so discipline silently does nothing). A deliberate, attributed raise for a permanent enforcement-observability surface backing the self-discipline directive (make Máddu enforce its own rituals) — not temporary debt. Nothing retired: no existing gate reports ritual freshness or hook wiring. Prior: 72→73 +skill-no-external-refs (auto-injectable skills locally resident — closes the audit-time half of the skill URL-swap attack surface); 71→72 (roadmap #12c phase 3) +replica-untracked; 70→71 (v1.94.0, #12b phase 7) +event-schema-complete.

verbs.note: top-level CLI verbs (bin/maddu.mjs COMMANDS). Raised 71->72 (SLM-governance phase 3, 2026-07-06, plan pln_20260706133422_0f60): +model — the SLM-factory governance verb (hash-pinned manifest ingest -> contract-1.1.0 MODEL_* events; promotion via an always-on stage-keyed approval ride; Maddu records the factory, never trains). Flagged as verb #72 in the phase-0 design doc (docs/research/slm-governance-design.md par.6/par.11) BEFORE the build — the EXP-phase-3 precedent. Nothing retired: no existing verb records ML-lifecycle manifests. Prior: 70->71 (EXP roadmap phase 3): +evolve, the recommend-only evolution planner; the companion experience verb consumed the last headroom under the old cap.

audit-checks.note: maddu audit coherence checks (reusable gates surfaced + audit-only checks)

selfTest.note: relative self-test latency ceiling. WARN (never FAIL) when the last recorded quick/full run in .maddu/state/self-test-last-run.json exceeds baselineMs * (1 + tolerancePct/100). Raise the baseline deliberately when the suite legitimately grows. Raised 36000→48000 then →115000 across v1.93.0 (roadmap #12a phases 1-5). Attribution (measured, not laundered): (a) `maddu ci` grew 5.4s→8.7s from the #12 surface additions (2 new event types, 3 new runtime libs, more source for the architecture gates) and the ci-command self-test invokes `maddu ci` 7× sequentially, so that +3.3s amplifies to ~+23s; (b) the two git-spawning integration tests worktree-attach + worktree-detach add ~11s; (c) the suite runs sequentially so per-test costs sum. The dominant driver is ci-command's 7× ci pattern — a candidate to parallelize or thin in a future pass.
